rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Implementation-level bindings should only use existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in assign statements.
generic_rules: AGREE guarantees in component type scopes must relate visible inputs to visible outputs; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths.
When AGREE diagnostics reference unresolved identifiers, trace and fix the root cause or remove the invalid clause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to prevent type resolution errors.
AGREE scalar types must be int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference valid output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type-level guarantees for visible port dependencies.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
